response.justification: While Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map work, the role's non-negotiable core is active commercial C++ or Rust development — neither of which the candidate demonstrates in any recent capacity. C++ last appeared professionally in 2014–2015 at National Instruments, over a decade ago. The current stack is overwhelmingly TypeScript and Node.js. There is no evidence of routing data pipelines, tiling systems, or navigation-guidance infrastructure at any scale, and the candidate is currently in a career gap.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application development spanning multiple years
Demonstrated distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O in a production microservices environment
Breadth of platform experience across embedded, mobile, web, and Linux environments, showing adaptability across the technical stack
concerns: C++ experience is over a decade old (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) with no indication of subsequent use; no Rust experience appears anywhere in the resume
No exposure to routing tiles, map data pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or tiling-optimized data formats — the core domain of the role
Professional focus for the past five-plus years has been web and TypeScript/Node.js, which is fundamentally misaligned with a systems-level C++ pipeline engineering role
